rationale: The citation is used in the General Discussion as more than generic background. The authors explicitly invoke Van den Bergh et al. (2011) as a concrete alternative mechanism (physical movements triggering approach motivation and vice choice), then state that their Study 3 results do not support this account in their setting. Because the cited paper is engaged as a specific competing explanation that is empirically assessed and discounted, the role is substantive.
relation_description: The citing paper considers Embodied Myopia as a plausible embodied-approach account for higher vice choice in offline shopping, but argues its own evidence does not support that mechanism as the driver of the observed channel effect. In this context, the citing paper uses the cited work as a contrasted explanation rather than a supported foundation.
